title: teams — subprocessors
description: The Teams module itself engages ZERO third-party subprocessors — every state transition happens within the Stackra platform. The Notifications module dispatches team + trial + coach-change notifications via its own subprocessors (mail, SMS, push); those are declared in notifications::subprocessors.json. The Finance module (Wave 4) will engage payment processors for trial fees + event registration fees. This file lists ZERO active subprocessors to be explicit about the module's boundary — team state is fully first-party.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.used_for: Dispatching TeamMemberJoined / TeamMemberLeft / TeamRegistrationOpening / TeamScheduleChanged / TrialScheduled / TrialDecision / TeamCoachChanged notifications via mail / SMS / push channels.
Payment processing for trial fees (TeamTrial.price_amount_cents) + Wave 3+ event registration fees (EventTeam-associated invoicing). Stripe / Paddle / Adyen depending on region.

notes: This file exists deliberately even with an empty subprocessors array. The GDPR Art. 30 record of processing activities requires per-module subprocessor lists; an empty list is the correct declaration for a module that engages none directly.

If a future Wave introduces a subprocessor into this module (e.g. an AI-driven talent-evaluation service that receives trial data), add it here + update compliance.json Art. 28 evidence + refresh the tenant-facing subprocessor list.
